req.title: Lab order routing (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-093 requires three verifiable dimensions: (1) happy-path routing to a patient's preferred lab when available, (2) fallback routing when the preferred lab is unavailable, and (3) boundary behaviour when the preferred lab list transitions from populated to empty. Each dimension also mandates an AuditEvent emission, so every test asserts both the ServiceRequest outcome and the corresponding AuditEvent. Tests are self-contained via fixture-injected auth tokens and ephemeral patient/lab resources that are cleaned up in teardown. Heuristics: EP covers the nominal routing partition, NEG covers the fallback/error partition, BVA covers the zero-preferred-labs edge, and STC is layered onto the positive test to walk the order through draft→active→completed states.

tests.name: test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Patient record exists with exactly one preferred lab set in extension preferredLab
Preferred lab Organization resource is active and reachable
FHIR server is running at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject=patient, performer=preferred_lab, intent=order, status=active
expected: ServiceRequest.performer[0].reference matches preferred lab Organization id
At least one AuditEvent exists with action='C' and outcome='0' (success)
trace: R-HLT-093: order routed to preferred lab
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_and_emits_audit(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    preferred_lab_org_id,    # <fixture>
    test_patient_id,         # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>
    cleanup_resources,       # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{test_patient_id}"},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{preferred_lab_org_id}"}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    sr = resp.json()
    sr_id = sr["id"]
    cleanup_resources.append(("ServiceRequest", sr_id))
    assert sr["performer"][0]["reference"] == f"Organization/{preferred_lab_org_id}"
    assert sr["status"] == "active"
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", "action": "C"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for ServiceRequest creation"
    audit = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert audit["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab that is marked inactive/unavailable
A default lab Organization resource is configured system-wide
FHIR server is running at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject=patient, performer=inactive_preferred_lab, intent=order, status=active
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 (server accepts and reroutes)
ServiceRequest.performer[0].reference matches default lab Organization id, NOT the inactive preferred lab
AuditEvent exists with action='C'; entity detail includes routing-fallback note
trace: R-HLT-093: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation including fallback
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(
    clinician_auth_headers,       # <fixture>
    inactive_preferred_lab_org_id, # <fixture> – lab set to status=inactive
    default_lab_org_id,           # <fixture>
    test_patient_id,              # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,                # <fixture>
    cleanup_resources,            # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{test_patient_id}"},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{inactive_preferred_lab_org_id}"}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    sr = resp.json()
    sr_id = sr["id"]
    cleanup_resources.append(("ServiceRequest", sr_id))
    actual_performer = sr["performer"][0]["reference"]
    assert actual_performer == f"Organization/{default_lab_org_id}", (
        f"Expected fallback to default lab, got {actual_performer}"
    )
    assert actual_performer != f"Organization/{inactive_preferred_lab_org_id}"
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ServiceRequest/{sr_id}"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for fallback routing"

tests.name: test_lab_order_with_empty_preferred_lab_list_routes_to_default_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Patient record exists with zero preferred labs (empty extension list — boundary: count=0)
Default lab Organization resource is active
FHIR server is running at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject=patient, no performer specified, intent=order, status=active
Assert performer resolved to default lab (server-side defaulting logic)
expected: ServiceRequest.performer[0].reference equals default lab Organization id
Exactly one AuditEvent with action='C' and outcome='0' exists
trace: R-HLT-093: boundary — zero preferred labs triggers default routing
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted even on default-only routing path
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_lab_order_with_empty_preferred_lab_list_routes_to_default_and_emits_audit(
    clinician_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    default_lab_org_id,       # <fixture>
    patient_no_preferred_lab, # <fixture> – patient with empty preferredLab extension
    fhir_base_url,            # <fixture>
    cleanup_resources,        # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_no_preferred_lab}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]}
        # NOTE: performer intentionally omitted — boundary: empty preferred list
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    sr = resp.json()
    sr_id = sr["id"]
    cleanup_resources.append(("ServiceRequest", sr_id))
    assert len(sr.get("performer", [])) == 1, "Expected exactly one performer after default assignment"
    assert sr["performer"][0]["reference"] == f"Organization/{default_lab_org_id}"
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", "action": "C"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "AuditEvent must exist for default-lab routing path"
    assert entries[0]["resource"]["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_lab_order_state_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed_with_audit
preconditions: Patient record exists with one active preferred lab
FHIR server supports ServiceRequest status lifecycle (draft→active→completed)
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with status=draft to create order
PUT /ServiceRequest/<id> with status=active to activate order
PUT /ServiceRequest/<id> with status=completed to complete order
expected: Three AuditEvents exist (one C + two U actions) each with outcome='0'
AuditEvent actions are ['C','U','U'] in chronological order
trace: R-HLT-093: status tracked across full lifecycle
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation (C and U)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_lab_order_state_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed_with_audit(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    preferred_lab_org_id,    # <fixture>
    test_patient_id,         # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>
    cleanup_resources,       # <fixture>
    build_service_request,   # <helper_fn>(patient_id, lab_id, status) -> dict
):
    # State 1: draft
    r1 = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=build_service_request(test_patient_id, preferred_lab_org_id, "draft"),
        headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    sr_id = r1.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resources.append(("ServiceRequest", sr_id))
    assert r1.json()["status"] == "draft"
    # State 2: active
    body_active = r1.json()
    body_active["status"] = "active"
    r2 = requests.put(f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}",
        json=body_active, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "active"
    # State 3: completed
    body_done = r2.json()
    body_done["status"] = "completed"
    r3 = requests.put(f"{fhir_base_url}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}",
        json=body_done, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "completed"
    # Audit coverage
    audit_resp = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", "_sort": "date"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(events) >= 3, f"Expected >=3 AuditEvents, got {len(events)}"
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in events[:3]]
    assert actions[0] == "C" and actions[1] == "U" and actions[2] == "U"
    assert all(e["resource"]["outcome"] == "0" for e in events[:3])
